Chin-ar..Chest: Anxiety in chest, region of heart. Constriction. Hemorrhage of lungs. Oppression of chest. Pain in the chest, during cough. Pains in the sides of the chest. Rawness in the chest. Stitching in the chest on coughing, Stitching in the heart. Palpitation of the heart, anxious, aggravated on slight exertion, leaning back against chair; violent. Sensation as if heart ceased to beat. Full pulse. Weakness in chest. Weakness of respiratory muscles.
Chin-ar..Chill: CHILL, morning, forenoon, noon, AFTERNOON evening, night, midnight; open air; walking in the open air; anticipating; in bed. Drinking aggravates chill. Quotidian chill; quotidian or tertian. Hard, shaking chill. Chill like cold waves with gooseflesh all over body. Warm room ameliorates. External warmth ameliorates.
Chin-ar..Generalities: Periodicity is most marked. Pulsation all over the body. Pulse fast, feeble and irregular. Relaxed and flabby (Calc.). Sensitive to pain. Many symptoms come during sleep. Standing increases many symptoms. Sensitive to touch. Trembling. Walking in the open air aggravates. Weakness from walking. Complaints come on in windy, stormy weather.
Chin-ar..Mind: Easily angered, and refuses to talk or to answer questions. Anxiety day and night, but worse in the evening, worse during chill; anxiety with fear. Anxiety during fever, even becomes wild. Anxiety on waking. Desires things which he cares nothing for after he gets them. Becomes critical with his most intimate friends. Complaining. Confusion of mind in the morning on waking.
Chin-ar..Mouth: Burning canker sores in the mouth. Bleeding from mucous membranes of the mouth. Cracked tongue, Tongue black, brown, white or yellow. Dry mouth and tongue. Hot mouth. Burning rawness of the tongue. Sore tongue. Salivation. Swollen gums and tongue. Taste bad, bitter while eating; insipid, metallic, saltish, sour, sweetish. Vesicles on the tongue.
Chin-ar..Rectum: Diarrhea, morning, afternoon, NIGHT, after midnight; after cold drinks; from taking cold; AFTER EATING; after fruit; in hot weather. Dysentery. Much flatus, offensive. Bleeding from anus. Hemorrhoids. Involuntary stool and urine. Itching of the anus. Moisture about the anus. Pain in the anus during stool. Burning in the anus during diarrhea, during stool. Pressing pain. Stitching. Paralytic weakness of the rectum. Ineffectual urging to stool.
Chin-ar..Stomach: Appetite diminished, or ravenous. No appetite for breakfast. Strong appetite without relish of food. Aversion to rich food and fats; aversion to food, to meat. Desires wine; cold drinks, sour things, sweet things. Sensation of coldness in the stomach. Emptiness, better by eating. Eructations after eating, bitter, empty, of food, sour. Water brash. Sensation of fullness after eating. Heartburn. Great weight in the stomach after eating. Hiccough. Stomach easily disordered.
Chin-ar..Stomach, pain: Pain in the stomach from coughing; after eating. Burning; cramping; pressing, soreness; stitching, tearing.
Chin-ar..Stomach, pain, pressing: Burning; cramping; pressing, soreness; stitching, tearing.
Chin-ar..Stool: Stool bilious, black, bloody, clay colored, copious, frequent, LIENTERIC, offensive, liquid, water. Diarrhea with intermittent fever.
Chin-ar..Stomach, vomiting: Vomiting at night, on coughing, after drinking, after eating, with headache; of bile black, blood, food, mucus; sour; water. Nausea and vomiting followed by sleep. Sudden inclination to vomit at 2 P.M.
Chin-ar..Mouth, tongue: Cracked tongue, Tongue black, brown, white or yellow. Dry mouth and tongue. Burning rawness of the tongue. Sore tongue. Swollen gums and tongue. Vesicles on the tongue.
Chin-ar..Expectoration: Expectoration bloody, copious; difficult; mucus, offensive, purulent; tastes bitter, flat, salty. Expectoration is viscid, white.
Chin-ar..Fever: High fever follows chill. Fever without chill afternoon and evening. Fever and chill alternate. Burning fever. Feverish all the time, but more so at night. Dry heat at night. Hectic fever. Heat during sleep. Chill, heat, then sweat. During the beat be desires to uncover.
Chin-ar..Nose: Coryza with discharge. Dry coryza. Nasal catarrh with bloody discharge, or purulent discharge. Dryness in the nose. Epistaxis. Nose obstructed. Sneezing. Excoriation of the corners of the nose. Especially useful for periodical coryza and frequent taking cold in the nose which keeps a catarrh in constant activity.
Chin-ar..Perspiration: Perspiration, morning, night; during anxiety. COLD; from coughing; with weakness; during slight exertion; following fever; from motion; PROFUSE; during sleep; after waking; staining linen yellow. Symptoms increase while perspiring.
Chin-ar..Perspiration, cold: COLD; from coughing; with weakness; during slight exertion; following fever; from motion; PROFUSE; during sleep; after waking; staining linen yellow.
Chin-ar..Perspiration, profuse: Perspiration, morning, night; during anxiety. COLD; from coughing; with weakness; during slight exertion; following fever; from motion; PROFUSE; during sleep; after waking; staining linen yellow.
Chin-ar..Rectum, diarrhea: Diarrhea, morning, afternoon, NIGHT, after midnight; after cold drinks; from taking cold; AFTER EATING; after fruit; in hot weather.
Vomiting at night, on coughing, after drinking, after eating, with headache; of bile black, blood, food, mucus; sour; water.
Chin-ar..Stool, lienteric: Stool bilious, black, bloody, clay colored, copious, frequent, LIENTERIC, offensive, liquid, water.
Chin-ar..Mouth, discoloration, tongue: Tongue black, brown, white or yellow.
Chin-ar..Stomach, vomiting, eating: Vomiting at night, on coughing, after drinking, after eating, with headache; of bile black, blood, food, mucus; sour; water.
Chin-ar..Stomach, vomiting, eating, after: Vomiting at night, on coughing, after drinking, after eating, with headache; of bile black, blood, food, mucus; sour; water.
Chin-ar..Chill, afternoon: CHILL, morning, forenoon, noon, AFTERNOON evening, night, midnight; open air; walking in the open air; anticipating; in bed.
Chin-ar..Expectoration, mucous: Expectoration bloody, copious; difficult; mucus, offensive, purulent; tastes bitter, flat, salty. Expectoration is viscid, white.
Chin-ar..Fever, night: Feverish all the time, but more so at night. Dry heat at night.

Cic..Back: Spasmodic symptoms of the back. Opisthotonos.
Cic..Back, pain: the head is drawn back opisthotonos all the limbs are convulsed and rigid.
Cic..Chest: Cold sensation in the chest. Spasms of the chest.
Cic..Extremities: All conditions of the limbs are of a spasmodic character.
Cic..Generalities: This remedy is of interest because of its convulsive tendency. It puts the whole nervous system in such a state of increased irritability that pressure on a part causes convulsions.
Cic..Head: Headache as if the brain were loose on walking. When thinking of the exact nature of the pain it ceased.
Cic..Mind: Wants to be alone; dislike to society. Singing, shouting, dancing; likes toys, jumps about like a child. Lies in bed lamenting and wailing. Great agitation; child grasps at one's clothing in a frightened manner.
Cic..Skin: It has troubles from shaving; it is useful in such eruptions as come in the whiskers; barber's itch; a solid crop of eruptions all over the face wherever the whiskers grow. Eruptions on the cheek like eczema.
Cic..Stomach: An aura in the stomach gives warning of the convulsion.
Cic..Stomach, distension: if the stomach is disordered or chilled, or if he has fear or other mental conditions, convulsions come on.
Cic..Stomach, pain: An aura in the stomach gives warning of the convulsion.
Cic..Abdomen, pain: With severe pains in the bowels come convulsive movements and convulsions.
Cic..Generalities, pain: there may be compression and yet all the pains be in distant parts; drawing of the muscles and cramps.
Cic..Appetite: This patient has strange desires; desires to eat coal and many other strange articles, because he is unable to distinguish between things edible and things unfit to be eaten; eats coal and raw potatoes.
Cic..Appetite, desires: This patient has strange desires; desires to eat coal and many other strange articles, because he is unable to distinguish between things edible and things unfit to be eaten; eats coal and raw potatoes.
Cic..Clinical: It has cured traumatic tetanus lockjaw, epilepsy, epileptiform convulsions.
Cic..Heart & Circulation, heart: Feels as if the heart stopped beating.
Cic..Abdomen: With severe pains in the bowels come convulsive movements and convulsions.
Cic..Back, dorsal region, back: Spasmodic symptoms of the back. Opisthotonos.
Cic..Heart & Circulation: Some complaints spread from the chest, especially from the heart; the rigors and chills begin in the chest; and there is a sensation of coldness about the heart; and from there it extends to other parts.
Cic..Back, dorsal region: Spasmodic symptoms of the back. Opisthotonos.
Cic..Face: Eruptions on the cheek like eczema. Swelling of the submaxillary glands. Erysipelatous eruptions.
Cic..Generalities, lying: He recollects nothing that took place or that he said during a certain period. He knows nobody, and lies without recognizing anyone, but when asked questions he answers correctly.
Cic..Mind, sadness: The Cicuta patient, out of the convulsion, is full of sadness, anxiety, and darkness, borrows trouble from the future, is affected by sad stories, is pessimistic.

Aspar..Extremities, coldness: There is coldness of the, extremities and also some itching of the skin, but the head symptoms are predominant.
Aspar..Mind, restlessness, nervousness: This patient is disturbed by everything, worse after eating even a moderate meal.
Aspar..Skin: The skin is sensitive to touch.
Aspar..Stomach, pain, gnawing: When there is gnawing in the stomach after eating, or when the child has taken all it can hold yet cries for the bottle, or empties its stomach by spitting up and vomiting the food and then reaches out whining and crying for more, think of Cina.
Aspar..Mind, night: The child takes a moderate supper and dreams all night, jerks and twitches in sleep, rouses up in a fright, talks excitedly about what he has dreamed, thinks it is real, and sees dogs, phantoms, and frightful things lie has dreamed about.
Aspar..Sleep, sleeplessness: Child cannot sleep unless on the belly or in constant motion.
Aspar..Stomach, heaviness, eating, after: The child or adult is not relieved by eating, is still hungry. The stomach is loaded and yet he is hungry.
Aspar..Stomach, heaviness, weight, oppression: The stomach is loaded and yet he is hungry.
Aspar..Stool: The Cina stool is not very copious, and often white.
Aspar..Generalities, heat: The complaints are aggravated in summer; the heat affects the brain, arrests his functions, and on comes diarrhoea with green, slimy stools or white stools, and the child vomits.
Aspar..Stomach, heaviness: The stomach is loaded and yet he is hungry.
Aspar..Stomach, heaviness, eating: The child or adult is not relieved by eating, is still hungry. The stomach is loaded and yet he is hungry.
Aspar..Extremities, coldness, foot: the head is hot and the feet are cold in the sun.
Aspar..Eye, brilliant: glassy eyes, drawn mouth and white ring around the nose and mouth.
Aspar..Clinical, cyanosis: Blue ring or gray streak around the mouth.
Aspar..Mind, fear: Screams and trembles, with much anxiety on waking; whines and complains.

Cinnb..Extremities: Pain in the limbs at night. Sensitive to sudden changes in the weather. Lame, bruised and stiff in all the limbs. Pains worse from motion. Syphilitic nodes on tibia.
Cinnb..Generalities: The symptoms are worse at night from warmth of the bed, and when perspiring, like Mercurius. Worse from both heat and cold,
Cinnb..Head: The pains in the head are violent; worse after eating; better by heat and pressure. Fullness in the whole head. Constriction. Pain in the cold forehead, which is ameliorated by heat. Tearing in the forehead before the menstrual flow. Pain in the forehead and vertex in the morning, worse lying on the left side and back, ameliorated lying on the right side, and it passes off after rising.
Cinnb..Head, congestion, vertex: Pain in the forehead and vertex in the morning, worse lying on the left side and back, ameliorated lying on the right side, and it passes off after rising.
Cinnb..Stomach: Aversion to food. Eructations, and vomiting. Tenderness of the stomach.
Cinnb..Clinical: Catarrhal inflammation. Fig warts (Thuja). Ulcers. Many complaints from eating. Syphilis in all stages. Suppurating glands; chancres.
Cinnb..Head, vertex: Pain in the forehead and vertex in the morning, worse lying on the left side and back, ameliorated lying on the right side, and it passes off after rising.
Cinnb..Genitalia male: Inflammation of glans penis with profuse secretion of pus. Increased sexual desire. Swelling of prepuce with much itching. Warts on the prepuce and fraenum, bleeding when touched. Chancres on the prepuce of gangrenous odor. Inflamed and swollen chancres, hard, discharging pus. Indurated, or neglected chancres.
Cinnb..Extremities, lameness: Lame, bruised and stiff in all the limbs.
Cinnb..Generalities, night: The symptoms are worse at night from warmth of the bed, and when perspiring, like Mercurius.
Cinnb..Head, forehead: Pain in the cold forehead, which is ameliorated by heat. Tearing in the forehead before the menstrual flow. Pain in the forehead and vertex in the morning, worse lying on the left side and back, ameliorated lying on the right side, and it passes off after rising.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, inflammation: Inflammation of glans penis with profuse secretion of pus. Inflamed and swollen chancres, hard, discharging pus.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, itching: Swelling of prepuce with much itching.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, swelling: Swelling of prepuce with much itching. Inflamed and swollen chancres, hard, discharging pus.
Cinnb..Head, fullness, forehead: Pain in the cold forehead, which is ameliorated by heat.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, penis: Inflammation of glans penis with profuse secretion of pus.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, glans: Inflammation of glans penis with profuse secretion of pus.
Cinnb..Head, fullness, vertex: Pain in the forehead and vertex in the morning, worse lying on the left side and back, ameliorated lying on the right side, and it passes off after rising.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, itching, penis: Swelling of prepuce with much itching.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, itching, penis, prepuce: Swelling of prepuce with much itching.
Cinnb..Genitalia male, prepuce: Swelling of prepuce with much itching. Warts on the prepuce and fraenum, bleeding when touched. Chancres on the prepuce of gangrenous odor.

Cist..Generalities: This remedy is an antipsoric, a deep-acting remedy. It runs very close to Calcarea, but is milder in its action. It has the same exhaustion from exertion, dyspnoea, sweating and coldness that we find in Calcarea.
Cist..Clinical: The glands inflame, become swollen and suppurate. It causes cries and cures old ulcers. It has a scrofulous constitution. It is useful in chronic diarrhea, with enlarged glands, even in those who are flabby, sickly and pallid and who cannot go upstairs without losing, their breath.
Cist..Ear: Shooting, stitching, tearing pains in inflamed parts. Red discharges from the ear date back to eruptive diseases.
Cist..Face: This remedy has cured lupus on the face. Caries of the lower jaw. Open, bleeding cancer on the lower lip. Lupus exedens. Pain in all the joints of the face.
Cist..Throat: The same cold feeling is described in the throat as in the nose-smarting and coldness. Mouth and throat full of mucus. The throat feels rough, as if full of sand. Dry spots in the throat. The throat looks glossy, shining as if vanished, in old atrophic catarrhs.
Cist..Generalities, heat: Hot air feels good everywhere. In old cases there is trouble with scrofulous glands, which are enlarged, and the patient wants the heat; goes to the register and turns the heat on, wants to feel the heat in the nose, throat and lungs.
Cist..Ear, discharges: Red discharges from the ear date back to eruptive diseases.
Cist..Clinical, inflammation: The glands inflame, become swollen and suppurate.
Cist..Clinical, swelling: The glands inflame, become swollen and suppurate. It causes cries and cures old ulcers. It has a scrofulous constitution.
Cist..External throat: Scrofula; swelling and suppuration of the glands of the neck.
It cures old, deep-seated, eating ulcers about the ankle and shin, with copious acrid discharge, formication and swollen glands, aggravation from bathing, extreme sensitiveness to the open air, only comfortable when very warm.
Cist..Clinical, swelling, glands: The glands of the neck are enlarged in lines, like knotted rope, as in Hodgkins disease.
Cist..Throat, dryness: Hawking of gum-like mucus, especially in the morning, fauces inflamed and dry. Suppuration of the glands of the throat.
Cist..Face, inflammation: Sharp shooting, intolerable itching and thick crusts, with burning on right zygoma.
Cist..Generalities, cold: Frontal headache with coldness. In a warm room the perspiration comes out, and it is cold, and the more he sweats the colder he gets.
Cist..External throat, swelling: Scrofula; swelling and suppuration of the glands of the neck.
Cist..External throat, swelling, cervical glan: The glands of the neck are enlarged in lines, like knotted rope, as in Hodgkins disease.
Cist..Generalities, heat, vital, lack of: They do not feel cold to touch, but they are cold subjectively, chilly.
Cist..External throat, glands: Suppuration of the glands of the throat. The glands of the neck are enlarged in lines, like knotted rope, as in Hodgkins disease.
Cist..External throat, glands, cervical: The glands of the neck were large and hard, the parotids especially.
Cist..Generalities, heat, vital: Patients going into tuberculosis have that desire for beat; chilly persons, They do not feel cold to touch, but they are cold subjectively, chilly.
Cist..Face, parotid glands: The parotid gland is so much enlarged that it pushes the head to one side.
Cist..Face, inflammation, parotid gland: The glands of the neck were large and hard, the parotids especially; she had foetid otorrhoea; her eyes were inflamed and suppurating.
Cist..Back: Eruption on the back like shingles. Scrofulous ulcer on the back. Burning, bruised pain in the coccyx, worse from touch.
Cist..Back, pain: Burning, bruised pain in the coccyx, worse from touch.
Cist..Chest: The chest fills up with mucus and he feels relieved after expectoration, but after he empties the chest it feels raw. Left mamma inflamed, suppurating, with a feeling of fullness in the chest.
Cist..Extremities: Tetter on the hands; blisters oozing after scratching. Diseases of the nails. Hard, thickened places on the hands of workmen, with deep cracks.
Cist..Extremities, coldness: It has the same exhaustion from exertion, dyspnoea, sweating and coldness that we find in Calcarea.

Clem..Bladder: Constant urging to urinate, most painful tenesmus. Flow stopping and starting. The urethra is painful to pressure. Micturition is remarkably slow, only a feeble stream because of the smallness of the urethra.
Clem..Chest: Scirrhus of the breast with induration and ulceration. Scirrhus of the left mamma with stitches in the shoulder;
Clem..Generalities: It has pain on lying down and preparatory to going to sleep. An electric shock; twitching, jerking, as if a faradic battery had been turned on. It has also a general febrile condition, but nothing very striking.
Clem..Head: On the outer head we have a part of the manifestations of Clematis vesicular eruptions with great itching, stinging and crawling.
Clem..Skin: It has vesicular eruptions almost erysipelatous in character. Vesicular eruptions filling with yellow fluid and with induration under the vesicles. It produces eruptions very closely related to herpes and eczema, and they spread.
Clem..Bladder, urination: Inability to evacuate all the urine at once. When beginning to urinate it burns the worst, while urinating it sticks in urethra, and after urinating it still continues to burn.
Clem..Generalities, side: While it has both sides, strange to say there is more trouble in the glands that are on the right side of the body than on the left. Strange to say, the right side of the body is more commonly affected than the left.
Clem..Clinical: It is seldom indicated in the very first stage of gonorrhoea during the highest inflammation, but in those cases that are inclined to hang on. Then come the sequelae if gonorrhea be suppressed. Inflammation of the testes is common, and this is one of the medicines suitable.
Clem..Abdomen: Swelling of the glands of the groin is a striking feature even when connected with scirrhus. It is connected also with suppressed gonorrhea, and with rheumatism of the joints.
Clem..Eye: Burning, and smarting of the eyes; worse from closing them. Inflammation of the iris. Eyes inflamed, protruded, dim.
Clem..Genitalia male: Pain and swelling of the right spermatic cord; this is worse at night, worse from walking and from the warmth of the bed. Pain in the testes, drawing. Painful, inflamed and swollen testes. Orchitis with much painful swelling and hardness.
Clem..Urine: Discharge from the urethra of thick pus. Intermittent flow of the urine. Urine stops and starts with the chordee still present.
Clem..Bladder, urination, involuntary: He always feels as if there was a little more, and when he appears to have finished it will keep dribbling away.
Clem..Eye, inflammation: Burning, and smarting of the eyes; worse from closing them. Inflammation of the iris. Eyes inflamed, protruded, dim.
Clem..Eye, inflammation, conjunctiva: About the eyes we have vesicular eruptions. If seen in one stage it will be vesicular, and if later, it will be seen as an ulceration.
Clem..Head, eruption: On the outer head we have a part of the manifestations of Clematis vesicular eruptions with great itching, stinging and crawling.
Clem..Head, eruption, crusts, scabs: On the outer head we have a part of the manifestations of Clematis vesicular eruptions with great itching, stinging and crawling. Now what is true of the eruption here will be true of the Clematis eruption everywhere. It is aggravated from washing.
Clem..Skin, eruptions: Eruption of vesicles and pustules from the former exuded a clear, watery secretion, from the latter a purulent fluid. Yellow vesicles and yellow pustules. Both are common to this remedy. Dark, burning eruptions with violent itching.
Clem..Skin, eruptions, herpetic: Vesicular eruptions on the body. Herpetic eruptions here and there it has a herpetic constitution. Herpes that ulcerate. Ichorous, spreading ulcers. Simple and graver forms of herpes. Herpes zoster about the body.
Clem..Teeth: Stitching and drawing pains in the teeth; worse at night; better for a short time from holding cold water in the mouth; better from drawing in cold air; worse from the warmth of the bed. The toothache is tolerable during the day, but as soon as he lies down in bed and assumes a horizontal position it increases to an intolerable degree.
Clem..Clinical, inflammation: Inflammation of the testes is common, and this is one of the medicines suitable. It is the nature of this remedy to infiltrate and inflame tissues, and, hence it is useful in those cases of gonorrhoea where they have been slow in passing away.

Cocc..Back, pain: The Cocculus headaches and backaches, pains and distress are present.
Cocc..Chest: The heart is weak, pulse feeble.
Cocc..Chill: Intermittent fevers with pains in the limbs, especially in the knees and bones of the legs, with that peculiar stiffness, nausea, and loathing of food.
Cocc..Extremities: Paralytic weakness in the limbs, numbness, jerking of the muscles, twitching, quivering, loss of sensation, loss of power, muscular weakness in all the limbs. Numbness and paralytic feeling in the limbs.
Cocc..Extremities, paralysis: Paralysis of the lower extremities, proceeding from the small of the back. Arising from cold, from the abuse of Mercury. Paralysis of the lower limbs, with stiffness, numbness and bruised feeling.
Cocc..Extremities, paralysis, painless: It was the case of a little girl with paralysis of lower extremities after diphtheria and no hope was given. But Doctor Moore (he was then an Octogenarian) looked over the case.
Cocc..Generalities: Cocculus slows down all the activities of the body and mind, producing a sort of paralytic weakness. Behind time in all its actions.
Cocc..Genitalia female: Copious menstrual flow, menses too soon; last too long. Catamenia two weeks before the time. In women prostrated from grief and from anxiety, and from prolonged loss of sleep, menses come too soon, are copious and prolonged.
Cocc..Genitalia female, pain: Violent, cramping pains in the bowels, clutching pains in the uterus during menstruation.
Cocc..Head: Congestion of the head, pressing, throbbing headache. Headache as if the skull would burst, or like a great valve opening and shutting. Sick headache with vertigo.
Cocc..Mind: You can hardly see what is coming when the mental activities are slowed down, from anxiety, and loss of sleep, such as we have in nursing. The mind appears like approaching imbecility, and as you look upon the true Cocculus case you wonder if that patient has not been growing insane for a year or two, because the mind seems almost a blank.
Cocc..Mouth: Metallic taste in the mouth. Bitter taste in the mouth. Sour, nauseous taste in the mouth, and no food tempts him.
Cocc..Rectum: A paralytic condition of the rectum. Inability to press at stool. Urging to stool and burning in rectum.
Cocc..Rectum, constipation: Disposition to stool, but peristaltic motion in upper intestines is wanting.
Cocc..Sleep: Sleeplessness from long nursing and from night watching; that is a symptom that I have called your attention to so often. Anxious, frightful dreams; ill effects from loss of sleep and night watching.
Cocc..Sleep, restless: Slightest loss of sleep tells on him.
Cocc..Stomach: Spasms of the stomach. Violent attacks of gastralgia, violent cramp of the stomach. Griping, pinching, constrictive pain.
Cocc..Stomach, pain: Violent attacks of gastralgia, violent cramp of the stomach. Griping, pinching, constrictive pain.
Cocc..Vertigo: The vertigo is visually attended with nausea. A Cocculus case cannot look out of the car window, cannot look down from the boat and see water moving, without nausea immediately.
Cocc..Abdomen, pain: The pain in the bowels feels as if the intestines were pinched between sharp stones. This causes fainting and vomiting. Colicky pains in the bowels great distension of the abdomen. Tearing, cutting, spasmodic pains in the bowels.
Cocc..Back, weakness: She is exhausted; unable to sustain any mental or physical effort; weak in the knees, weak in the back.
Cocc..Generalities, faintness: Faintness on moving the body, fainting from pain in the bowels, from colic.
Cocc..Generalities, pain: With all this slowing down of the thoughts and activities the patient remains extremely sensitive to suffering, sensitive to pain.
Cocc..Clinical, paralysis: I remember a case of paralysis of the lower extremities that was prescribed for by a very careful homeopathic physician many years ago. He studied the case carefully and gave Cocculus c.m. It was not many days before the child began to move the legs, and the condition was perfectly cleared up.
Cocc..Generalities, weakness: He cannot endure any muscular exertion, because he is weak; he is tired. First comes this slowness, then a sort of visible paralytic condition, and then complete paralysis.

Coc-c..Bladder: Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
Coc-c..Generalities: The patient is cold, and his complaints come on in cold weather. He is sensitive to cold, easily takes cold. But you must distinguish between the patient himself and his complaints, because they are entirely opposite to each other. When he once becomes sick from exposure to cold, he is always worse in a warm room and better in the cold air.
Coc-c..Genitalia female: Uterine hemorrhage is a strong feature of this remedy. Copious, frequent, prolonged menstrual flow. Large, hard, black clots fill the uterus, are expelled by labor-like pains, and form again. Inflammation of uterus and vagina, with copious, thick, white, jelly-like, ropy mucus. Soreness of the vulva; cannot bear the pressure of the clothing.
Coc-c..Generalities, heat, sensation of: There is a general hyperesthesia of the skin and mucous membranes. Sensitive to the pressure of the clothing.
Coc-c..Throat: Sore throat with redness. Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx. Fauces very sensitive. Arch of palate and fauces, as far as visible, very red. Burning in the throat < in the warmth, especially when warmed up in bed, < from warm drinks, though hot drinks are not so bad. Better from cold drinks.
Coc-c..Generalities, heat: His cough is brought on in a warm room; from being too warm in bed; from drinking warm things. It is better from drinking cold things in a cold room; worse from exertion; from getting heated up; from becoming warm; that is, after the complaint has once set in, it reverses itself.
Coc-c..Genitalia female, menses: Copious, frequent, prolonged menstrual flow. Large, hard, black clots fill the uterus, are expelled by labor-like pains, and form again.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Urine: Albumen in the urine. Dark red sediment in the urine. Pain shooting from the kidney to the bladder and down the legs; < from motion.
Coc-c..Cough: Spasmodic cough; whooping cough; the cough of drunkards. The chronic catarrhal state of the Coccus cacti patient comes on especially in the winter. It comes on when the cold weather begins and lasts till the warm weather comes.
Coc-c..Expectoration: Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke. This is due to the fact that the mucus is so tenacious that it cannot be ejected from the pharynx in the usual way, therefore he must vomit.
Coc-c..Cough, tickling: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Expectoration, viscid: Great quantities of this mucus form in the nose, in the throat, in the air passages generally, and in the vagina. Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, irritation, larynx: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. The slightest touch on the palate or even the gums in examination of the throat will cause gagging sometimes when the parts look normal.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, tickling in the air: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Nose: Discharge of thick yellow mucus from the nose; nose stopped up, with inclination to sneeze. Great dryness of the nose.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, irritation: Anything coming in contact with the pharynx, the inside of the mouth, or even the gums, produces gagging and retching and will bring on the cough.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, tickling: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Larynx and trachea, larynx in general: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx.
Coc-c..Bladder, urging to urinate (morbid desir: Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
Urging to urinate, but inability to pass urine until a large clot of blood has been passed.
Coc-c..Cough, hacking, tickling in larynx, from: Tickling in the throat. Sensation of a hair or crumb lodged in the throat behind the larynx. He cannot hawk without gagging.
Coc-c..Cough, irritation in air passages, from: Anything coming in contact with the pharynx, the inside of the mouth, or even the gums, produces gagging and retching and will bring on the cough.
Coc-c..Cough, irritation in air passages, from,: If the patient gets warm in bed or the room gets warm he commences to clutch the larynx and cough.
Coc-c..Cough, night: They are apt to be worse at night when he becomes warm in bed. If he can lie in a cool room without much covering he will go longer without coughing.
Coc-c..Cough, paroxysmal: It is a spasmodic cough, forcing the patient into the most violent efforts. The face becomes purple. Finally he retches and vomits long strings of tough, ropy mucus filling the mouth and throat and causing him to choke.

Coff..Chest: Strong, quick palpitation of the heart with extreme nervousness, sleeplessness and cerebral erethism caused by unexpected news of great good fortune.
Coff..Chill: He is extremely sensitive to cold, sensitive to the wind and cold weather. Complaints come on in the cold weather, from the cold air.
Coff..Extremities: Coffea has a painful sensitiveness of the skin beyond comprehension. I remember one particular case. A woman had her lower limb out of bed and it was as red as fire down one side.
Coff..Generalities: This drug is characterized by a general sensitivity. Sensitiveness of vision, of hearing, of smell, of touch; sensitiveness to pain.
Coff..Head: Headache as if the whole brain were torn and bruised, or dashed to pieces. Worse from motion, noise or light.
Coff..Mind: Fainting from sudden emotions. Hysteria, nervousness, weeping. Pitiful weeping from pain trembling and weeping from hurt feelings; the slightest neglect. The greatest mental and physical exhaustion; great restlessness lying awake most of the night.
Coff..Mind, irritability: Pitiful weeping from pain trembling and weeping from hurt feelings; the slightest neglect.
Coff..Mouth: Pain in the mouth and jaws, better from holding ice-cold water in the mouth.
Coff..Rectum: The first effect of Opium is to constipate. Let several doses be given, and as the effects of the Opium wear off he may have diarrhea.
Coff..Sleep: The wakefulness produced by Coffea is well known, even to the laity. It is taken by nurses to keep them awake nights with their patients. The Coffea patient is quick to act and to think. So full of ideas that she lies awake nights making plans, thinking of a thousand things; utterly unable to banish the thoughts that flood the mind.
Coff..Generalities, faintness: Her heart palpitates, her pulse flickers, she has fainting spells, and without Coffea she may die.
Coff..Generalities, pain: Pains are increased by noise. Sensitiveness of hearing is so great that sounds are painful. Pains in the face, toothache, headache; pains in the lower limbs; everywhere aggravated by noise.
Coff..Head, congestion: At times the head is hot, the face is flushed and, the eyes so brilliant that one fears apoplexy.
Coff..Mind, restlessness: The greatest mental and physical exhaustion; great restlessness lying awake most of the night.
Coff..Clinical: Neuralgic toothache entirely relieved by holding cold water in the mouth, returning as it becomes warm. Toothache during the menstrual period. Complaints of anemic children during dentition.
Coff..Abdomen: Worse from every motion, with violent pain in the groins, and fear of death.
Coff..Heart & Circulation: Palpitation of the heart, pulse fluttering. Strong, quick palpitation of the heart with extreme nervousness, sleeplessness and cerebral erethism caused by unexpected news of great good fortune.
Coff..Face: The neuralgia of the face is a common feature of old coffee drinkers.
Coff..Face, heat: Eyes brilliant; pupils dilated; face flushed head hot.
Coff..Generalities, heat, sensation of: Intense stinging, burning pains in the skin with the redness and heat with coarse rash coming on suddenly, leaving just as suddenly.
Coff..Head, pain: Headache as if the whole brain were torn and bruised, or dashed to pieces. Worse from motion, noise or light. Headache intolerable. Head feels small, and as if filled with fluid. Nervous hysterical headache. One-sided headache.
Coff..Sleep, sleeplessness: So full of ideas that she lies awake nights making plans, thinking of a thousand things; utterly unable to banish the thoughts that flood the mind; hears the clocks on the distant steeples.
Coff..Generalities, heat: Hot head; inflamed condition of the gums. The head is hot, the face is flushed.

Colch..Chest: Stinging, tearing pains in the muscles of the chest.
Colch..Heart & Circulation, palpitation: Palpitation; oppression of the chest. Feels as if he had a great weight on the chest; cannot breathe.
Colch..Chill: He is a chilly patient, sensitive to cold.
Colch..Extremities: Numbness, oedema, swelling of the limbs.
Colch..Extremities, paralysis: Paralysis of the limbs or of one limb, or of any part.
Colch..Generalities: All the complaints of this remedy are aggravated from motion. The painful complaints, the head complaints, the bowel complaints. the liver complaints, the stomach complaints, are all worse from motion.
Colch..Generalities, weakness, enervation: Great weakness and exhaustion. He cannot exert himself in the least without causing dyspnea, Must lie down; does not want to move; sinking of strength; seems as if his life will flow out of him from motion and from exertion; so tired and exhausted.
Colch..Head: The headaches are all of a rheumatic character. Very often the whole skull, the pericranium, is sore as if bruised. The scalp is sensitive. Pressure in the head- constriction; pressing, bursting headaches. Heat in the head. Tearing in the scalp. Headaches are all aggravated by motion.
Colch..Mind: Colchicum has delirium, prostration, depression of mind, great sensitiveness to pain, which he seems to feel in his mind, and it brings out mental symptoms. Very sensitive to pain; confusion of the mind; disorders of comprehension. Cannot understand what he reads.
Colch..Rectum: Great relaxation of the parts. Protrusion of the rectum.
Colch..Rectum, pain: Very painful, extremely painful is the Colchicum stool. Great soreness in the abdomen.
Colch..Stomach: Nausea, gagging, retching at the bare mention of food in his presence. The thought and smell of food bring on nausea and vomiting.
Colch..Appetite, aversion, meat: The smell of fish, eggs, fat meats or broths causes nausea even unto faintness.
Colch..Stomach, distension: The abdomen is distended with flatus, tympanitic. Great soreness in the whole abdomen just such a tympanitic condition as we have in typhoid.
Colch..Stomach, pain: In the stomach there is sometimes coldness and sometimes burning.
Colch..Stomach, pain, burning: Burning in the pit of the stomach.
Colch..Stomach, thirst: The Colchicum patient may have much thirst or no thirst, or these may alternate.
Colch..Abdomen, pain: Spasmodic pains, colic, tearing pains, burning, griping pains, forcing the patient to bend double. Aggravated from motion. Great tenderness and soreness with the colic. Aggravated from eating; ameliorated from bending double.
Colch..Generalities, faintness: The smell of fish, eggs, fat meats or broths causes nausea even unto faintness.
Colch..Generalities, pain: Touch and motion bring on a painful sensation in the body as of electric vibrations.
Colch..Clinical, paralysis: A paralytic weakness was observed in the poisonous effects and too prolonged provings. The jaw hangs down, the muscles are flabby, relaxed. Paralysis of the limbs or of one limb, or of any part.
Colch..Generalities, weakness: Great prostration accompanies the complaints of this remedy. Weakness of the limbs, great exhaustion, nervous exhaustion of a typhoid character. He gradually grows weaker like one going into Bright's disease.
Colch..Appetite: Absolutely unable to touch food. Nausea, gagging, retching at the bare mention of food in his presence.
Colch..Appetite, aversion: Aversion to food; loathing the sight and smell, more the smell of it.
Colch..Clinical: Rheumatic troubles that have been going on for some time, will end in cardiac troubles. When cardiac troubles with valvular defects are present, almost the first thing the busy doctor thinks of is a history of rheumatism.
Colch..Heart & Circulation, heart: Heart's action muffled, indistinct, very weak.

Coloc..Abdomen, hard: The pains in the lower part of the abdomen are relieved by drawing up the limbs and pressing with the fists.
Coloc..Back: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
Coloc..Back, pain: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
Coloc..Back, pain, dorsal region: Anger will be followed by violent neuralgia in the head, eyes, down the spine and in the intestines.
Coloc..Extremities: Where the pain is better from hard pressure and from heat, where it is worse during repose and drives the patient to despair, Colocynth will generally cure.
Coloc..Generalities: The principal feature of Colocynth is its severe, tearing, neuralgic pains; so severe that the patient is unable to keep still. Sometimes they are > by motion - at least it appears that they are worse during rest- > by pressure and sometimes > by heat.
Sometimes they are > by motion - at least it appears that they are worse during rest- > by pressure and sometimes > by heat.
Coloc..Genitalia female: In the violent ovarian neuralgias of Colocynth, the woman will flex the limb of the painful side hard against the abdomen and hold it there.
Coloc..Genitalia female, pain: In the violent ovarian neuralgias of Colocynth, the woman will flex the limb of the painful side hard against the abdomen and hold it there.
Coloc..Head: Painful, tearing, digging through the whole brain, becoming unbearable when moving the eyelids. Intense pain through the whole head; worse from moving the eyes. Severe, pressing, tearing headache, causing her to cry out.
Coloc..Mind: As soon as the prover of Colocynth begins to have pains along the course of nerves he becomes irritable; everything vexes him; he is worse from vexation.
Coloc..Mind, irritability: As soon as the prover of Colocynth begins to have pains along the course of nerves he becomes irritable; everything vexes him; he is worse from vexation.
Coloc..Mind, restlessness, nervousness: Walks about the room and becomes increasingly anxious as the pain goes on.
Coloc..Rectum: Diarrhoea and dysentery from anger with indignation; the most awful tenesmus during stool; urging to stool with colic.
Coloc..Stomach: Aversion to food. Violent thirst. Colic brought on from drinking while overheated; from eating indigestible things, from high living; colic from eating potatoes.
Coloc..Stomach, pain: The stomach pains are clutching, cramping and digging; as if grasped by the fingers.
Coloc..Abdomen, pain: Similar pains occur lower down in the abdomen, but they are still better from hard pressure, and from doubling up which amounts to pressure, come on in paroxysms of increasing severity, until the patient is nauseated and vomits, and are associated with great restlessness and faint, sinking feeling at the pit of the stomach.
Coloc..Generalities, pain: The principal feature of Colocynth is its severe, tearing, neuralgic pains; so severe that the patient is unable to keep still.
Coloc..Generalities, pressure: All pains are better from pressure, but this is in the beginning. After the pain has been going for several days with increasing severity, the part becomes very sensitive and pressure cannot be endured.
Coloc..Generalities, side: They are generally on the left side; while those of Belladonna are on the right, and are caused by cold.
Coloc..Mind, restlessness: In spite of extreme restlessness there is great weakness with the pains.

Con..Back: In the back, weakness is a most striking thing, with some dorsal pains, Lancinating pains are spoken of.
Con..Back, pain: After injuries, especially in the lumbar region, pains and filling up of the veins of the lower limbs.
Con..Bladder: The bladder is weak, can expel only a part of the urine. Or sometimes there is a paralytic condition and no expulsive power.
Con..Chest: In the chest, violent stitches. Painful swelling of the breasts. Rending, tearing pains in the chest.
Con..Heart & Circulation, palpitation: After every stool tremulous weakness and palpitation.
Con..Extremities: Rheumatic pains; paralysis of the lower limbs; ulceration. And the sufferings and conditions are better by letting the limbs hang down.
Con..Extremities, paralysis: Paralysis of the legs and hip.
Con..Extremities, paralysis, painless: A great many of the complaints are painless. The ulcers and the paralytic conditions are painless.
Con..Generalities: Trembling, jerking of the muscles and twitching from the weakness of the nerves. Inability to stand any physical effort without great exhaustion.
Con..Generalities, weakness, enervation: Gradually growing paralytic weakness, somewhat as was described in Cocculus. Exhaustion of body and mind, that is a general slowing down of all the activities of the body.
Con..Genitalia female: In the woman induration and enlargement of the ovaries and uterus.
Con..Genitalia female, pain: Burning, stinging, tearing pains in the neck of the uterus.
Con..Head: There are many headaches in these patients. Patients going into decline will manifest headaches. Stitching, tearing pains in the head; throbbing in the head.
Con..Mind: Conium has such a deep action that it gradually brings about a state of imbecility. The mind gives out. The mind at first becomes tired like the muscles of the body. Unable to sustain any mental effort. The memory is weak.
Con..Mind, irritability: Every thing vexes and disturbs him. Cannot endure any kind of excitement, it brings on physical and mental distress, brings on weakness and sadness.
Con..Rectum: Often more common than diarrhoea is constipation with ineffectual urging, hard cool, paralysis of the rectum.
Con..Rectum, constipation: Often more common than diarrhoea is constipation with ineffectual urging, hard cool, paralysis of the rectum. Inability to strain at cool, inability to expel contents because of the paralytic weakness of all the muscles that take part in expulsion.
Con..Rectum, pain: Pulsation and emptiness in the abdomen after a normal stool.
Con..Skin: Nodules in the breast even where milk has not yet formed; lumps and nodules, indurations and enlarged glands form under the skin all over the body.
Con..Sleep: Another grand feature of the remedy; he sweats copiously during sleep. Sometimes the patient will say that if he merely closes the eyes he will sweat. It is certainly true on closing the eyes preparatory to going to sleep he will break out in a sweat.
Con..Stomach: There are many stomach troubles; ulceration of the stomach; cancer of the stomach. Conium is one of the greatest palliatives in symptoms of the stomach when all the symptoms agree.
Con..Stomach, eructations: Fullness in throat with suppressed eructations.
Con..Stomach, pain: Hardness of the abdomen, great sensitiveness of the abdomen. Pinching pains, stitching pains, colicky, cutting pains, cramping pains.
Con..Vertigo: Vertigo when turning the head, like turning in a circle, when rising from a seat; worse when lying down, as though the bed were turning in a circle; when turning in bed or when looking around.

Crot-h..Back: Carbuncles that come upon the neck and upon the back begin with a pustule, and then several come and they are surrounded by little pustules and papules and there is pitting upon pressure.
Crot-h..Generalities: An awful state of nervousness prevails. Trembling of the limbs, tremulous weakness. On protruding the tongue it comes out quivering. Tired by the slightest exertion. Sudden prostration of the vital powers. A paralytic weakness prevails throughout.
Crot-h..Generalities, faintness, fainting: A mental and physical prostration that is almost paralytic in character.
Crot-h..Generalities, side, right: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting.
Crot-h..Genitalia female: Hemorrhages, Either dark clots or blood that has no tendency to coagulate and keeps on flowing. There is great trouble at the climacteric period. Hot flashes. Jaundice. Hemorrhage from the uterus or from other parts. Cancer of the uterus with much bleeding.
Crot-h..Mouth: Inflammation of the gums. Bleeding from the mouth. Inflammation of the throat with bleeding of the throat. Burning in the throat and mouth, Trembling, quivering and swollen tongue. Trembling of the tongue when it is put out.
Crot-h..Rectum: Stool black, thin, like coffee grounds. Dysentery of septic origin from foul water, food, etc. Diarrhoea from noxious effluvia.
Crot-h..Skin: Boils, carbuncles and eruptions are surrounded by a purplish condition of the skin, a mottled, blue, splotched or marbled state. It produces boils, abscesses and a condition somewhat resembling a carbuncle, with burning and violent pains, but the peculiar feature is the doughy centre. Around the boil or carbuncle for many inches there is oedema, with pitting upon pressure.
Crot-h..Stomach: Pain in the stomach; coldness as if a piece of ice were in the stomach or in the abdomen. Stomach irritable, unable to retain anything, constantly throws up blood.
Crot-h..Generalities, faintness: A mental and physical prostration that is almost paralytic in character. Sudden prostration of the vital powers.
Crot-h..Generalities, side: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting.
Crot-h..Generalities, weakness: Tremulous weakness. Tired by the slightest exertion. Sudden prostration of the vital powers. A paralytic weakness prevails throughout.
Crot-h..Appetite: He craves intoxicating drinks and is unable to resist the craving. This wonderful resemblance to old inebriates has led to the use of Crotalus in delirium tremens; it has the besotted countenance, the purple aspect of the face, the peculiar kind of hunger in the drunkard, the craving by spells for stimulants.
Crot-h..Appetite, desires: He craves intoxicating drinks and is unable to resist the craving.
Crot-h..Clinical: Crotalus is indicated in disease of the very lowest, the most putrid type, coming on with unusual rapidity, reaching that putrid state in an unusually short time. Crotalus has cured ulceration of the stomach. It has greatly restrained the growth of carcinoma when there is much vomiting of bile and blood.
Crot-h..Heart & Circulation: There is some reason to think that this will be more or less a heart remedy from the great cardiac weakness it produces. But the other snake poisons like Naja, Lach. and Elaps have had more clinical application than this one. This one seems to prostrate the heart, but also to prostrate the whole body.
Crot-h..Ear: Surging in the ears. Sensitive to noises. Dull aching and throbbing in the ears. Foetid, copious, yellow, offensive, bloody discharges from the ears.
Crot-h..Stool: Stool black, thin, like coffee grounds. Dysentery of septic origin from foul water, food, etc. Diarrhoea from noxious effluvia.
Crot-h..Throat: Inflammation of the throat with bleeding of the throat. Burning in the throat and mouth. The throat will be filled up under such circumstances with a diphtheritic membrane that looks dark. There is bleeding all around it.
Crot-h..Ear, discharges: Foetid, copious, yellow, offensive, bloody discharges from the ears. Blood oozing from the ears in drops in zymotic diseases, low forms of scarlet fever, or of diphtheria where there is oozing from the eyes and ears.
Crot-h..Eye: Hemorrhage from the eyes. Yellow appearance of the eyes. Blood exudes from the eye, burning in eyes; redness with lachrymation. Pressure in the eyes as if the eyes would be pushed out from the head. Paralysis of the upper lids.
Crot-h..Mouth, protruded, tongue: Trembling, quivering and swollen tongue. Trembling of the tongue when it is put out.
Crot-h..Stomach, nausea: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting. This is a wonderfully bilious remedy, sick headaches, vomiting of bile in great quantities.
Crot-h..Stomach, vomiting: Cannot lie on the right side or back without instantly producing black, bilious vomiting. The various low forms of disease calling for Crotalus often, begin with vomiting great quantities of bile, sometimes bile mixed with blood. Stomach irritable, unable to retain anything, constantly throws up blood.

Croto-t..Chest: After confinement the mother may go on a little while with all things following normally, but all at once she commences to have pains in one or the other mammary gland, and the drawing as with a string comes up again. It feels to her as if a string were attached behind the nipple pulling backward, a sharp, drawing, stinging pain that will in some instances keep her walking the floor night and day.
Croto-t..Generalities: When one has been overdosed, as is done in a too prolonged proving, or by the crude drug, or when the prover has been markedly sensitive, we get an alternation of states, the internal alternating with the external. After the eruption is out the internal manifestations are not present, as is seen in the rheumatic state, the cough and the bowel symptoms.
Croto-t..Head: Troublesome eczema of the scalp in infants, either purely vesicular or intermingled more or less with pustules. The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch.
Croto-t..Rectum: pressure over the abdomen or pressure about the umbilicus causes a pain in the rectum and urging to stool, and a feeling, with the expulsion of the stool, as if the rectum would protrude.
Croto-t..Rectum, pain: pressure over the abdomen or pressure about the umbilicus causes a pain in the rectum and urging to stool, and a feeling, with the expulsion of the stool, as if the rectum would protrude. Clinically it has been described as if the pain followed the intestines all the way down to the anus.
Croto-t..Respiration: Attacks of violent coughing, with dyspnoea and choking, worse at night and worse on lying down, compelling him to sit up, to be bolstered up in bed, or to sit up in a reclining chair.
Croto-t..Respiration, difficult: It has an asthmatic cough, coming on in the middle of the night, often arousing the patient from a sound sleep. Attacks of violent coughing, with dyspnoea and choking, worse at night and worse on lying down, compelling him to sit up, to be bolstered up in bed, or to sit up in a reclining chair.
Croto-t..Skin: Croton oil, when applied to the skin, produces both vesicles and pustules upon an inflamed base, and the part becomes very red and sore. The inflammation often increases until it resembles erysipelas, but more commonly the eruption produced resembles a vesicular eczema.
Croto-t..Stomach: Excessive nausea with vanishing of sight, vertigo, worse after drinking, with frequent discharges of yellowish-green water from the bowels; excessive nausea, much water in the mouth. So we note the excessive nausea and not so great vomiting.
Croto-t..Abdomen, pain: With this the abdomen is very sensitive, and is greatly distended; there is much gurgling in the bowel. Another peculiar thing commonly attending Croton tig. diarrheas is that pressure over the abdomen or pressure about the umbilicus causes a pain in the rectum and urging to stool.
Croto-t..Clinical: If the diarrhoea becomes chronic the external eruption will disappear, and if the diarrhea improves the external eruption gets worse. It seems necessary in such a constitution to have a vent. The mucous membrane is but the internal skin, and the integument of the body the external skin, and this remedy especially manifests itself upon one or the other of these, the mucous membrane or the integument.
Croto-t..Abdomen: With this the abdomen is very sensitive, and is greatly distended; there is much gurgling in the bowel, and when the physician puts his hand upon it the patient will say he feels the gurgling, as if he were full of water.
Croto-t..Chest, pain: It feels to her as if a string were attached behind the nipple pulling backward, a sharp, drawing, stinging pain that will in some instances keep her walking the floor night and day.
Croto-t..Face: The eruptions are often about the eyes, on the temples, over the face and on top of the head.
Croto-t..Stool: one gush of yellow, watery or pappy stool; soft, thin faeces, coming, out with one gush, so marked is this that it is not an uncommon thing for a rural patient to describe it as "like that of a goose." It all gushes out in one squirt.
Croto-t..Genitalia male: "Frequent, corrosive itching on glans and scrotum." "Vesicular eruption on scrotum and penis." It is a remedy for vesicular and pustular eruptions upon the genital organs.
Croto-t..Stomach, vomiting: in Croton tig., the stools are copious and while there is nausea the vomiting is seldom and scanty.
Croto-t..Head, eruption: Troublesome eczema of the scalp in infants, either purely vesicular or intermingled more or less with pustules. The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch. After desquamation has pretty nearly finished, a new crop of pustules and vesicles comes out, and while one place is clearing off another is vesicular.
Croto-t..Head, eruption, crusts, scabs: The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch. After desquamation has pretty nearly finished, a new crop of pustules and vesicles comes out.
Croto-t..Rectum, diarrhea: It is suitable in both acute and chronic diarrhoea. It is suitable in cholera infantum. The marked feature is the extreme, suddenness with which the stool is ejected.
Croto-t..Skin, eruptions: the eruption produced resembles a vesicular eczema. This eruption will come on for a few days and will then desiccate, and in a few days longer it will desquamate.
Croto-t..Stool, copious: in Croton tig., the stools are copious and while there is nausea the vomiting is seldom and scanty.
Croto-t..Stool, thin, liquid: this violent gush of thin, yellow faeces or yellow water is a striking feature of the remedy.
Croto-t..Head, eruption, crusts: The vesicles dry up and then desquamate, and now there is a red, raw, inflamed surface, sensitive to touch.
Croto-t..Stool, thin: soft, thin faeces, coming, out with one gush
Croto-t..Abdomen, gurgling: there is much gurgling in the bowel, and when the physician puts his hand upon it the patient will say he feels the gurgling, as if he were full of water, and it probably is so, for the expulsion of the stool would not occur in one strong gush were it not for the fact that the colon and rectum were full of fluid.
Croto-t..Abdomen, pain, extending: Clinically it has been described as if the pain followed the intestines all the way down to the anus.
Croto-t..Abdomen, pain, umbilicus: pressure about the umbilicus causes a pain in the rectum and urging to stool

Cupr..Back: In the lower part of the chest, in the region of the xiphoid appendix, there is a spasmodic condition that is very troublesome. It seems to be at times a constriction so severe that he thinks he will die, and at others a feeling as if he were transfixed with a knife from the xiphoid appendix to the back.
Cupr..Back, pain: a feeling as if he were transfixed with a knife from the xiphoid appendix to the back.
Cupr..Chest: Cramping of the muscles of the chest; cramping of the calves; cramping all over. Spasms of the chest; spasms of the larynx; spasms of the whole respiratory system of such a character that the child seems to be choking to death. Constriction across the chest, suffocation, cramps of the legs.
Cupr..Chill: the extremities are cold, there is jerking of the muscles, cramping of the extremities and of the fingers and toes, spasms of the chest; he is cold, mottled, blue in blotches, going into collapse; the finger nails and toe nails and the hands and feet are blue.
Cupr..Extremities: In the fingers and toes and in the extremities the spasmodic condition increases and extends until the limbs are in a state of great exhaustion. Tonic contractions, the limbs being drawn up with great violence and it seems as if the frame would be torn to pieces by the violent contractions of the muscles everywhere.
Cupr..Extremities, coldness: the extremities are cold, there is jerking of the muscles, cramping of the extremities and of the fingers and toes, spasms of the chest; he is cold, mottled, blue in blotches, going into collapse; the finger nails and toe nails and the hands and feet are blue.
Cupr..Generalities: Convulsive cramps all over the body with twitching, jerking, trembling and blueness of the skin. Everything he does, all his actions are spasmodic, are convulsive. All the sphincters are convulsive, All the activities are irregular, disorderly and convulsive when poisoned with copper.
Cupr..Genitalia female: In spasmodic conditions that come on during menstruation Cuprum is also useful. Painful menstruation with spasms commencing in the fingers and extending over the body. Tonic contractions that look like hysterical manifestations. Violent dysmenorrhoea with delirium, turning up of the eyes, contortions of the face and epileptiform manifestations.
Cupr..Genitalia female, pain: Before or during menses, or after suppression, violent, unbearable cramps in the abdomen. Painful menstruation with spasms commencing in the fingers and extending over the body.
Cupr..Head: Violent congestion in the head, violent pains in the head. Tingling pain in the vertex, severe pain in the vertex, bruised pain. Crawling sensation in vertex, stitches in the temples. Congestion of the brain, Meningitis. Headache after epileptic attacks.
Cupr..Mind: Cuprum has many mental symptoms. It has a great variety in its delirium, incoherent prattling, talking of all sorts of subjects incoherently. It has produced a variety of mental symptoms; delirium, incoherency of speech, loss of memory.
Cupr..Mind, restlessness, nervousness: In the Cuprum patient the nerves are all the time wrought up to the highest tension; wants to fly, wants to do something dreadful. Impulsiveness. Compelled to do something; restless and tossing about a constant uneasiness; nervous trembling; always tired.
Cupr..Mouth: Inflammation of the tongue. Paralysis of the tongue, It is not an uncommon thing to find paralysis in Cuprum after convulsions, The violence of the convulsions seems to have brought about a reaction and paralytic weakness, a numbness and tingling, a loss of motility.
Cupr..Rectum: In cholera morbus with gushing, watery stools and copious vomiting, the stomach and bowels are emptied of their contents. The patient is fairly emptied out.
Cupr..Respiration: Whenever the respiratory organs are affected there is spasmodic breathing, dyspnoea. There is also rattling in the chest. The more dyspnoea there is the more likely his thumbs will be clenched and the fingers cramped.
Cupr..Respiration, difficult: Spasmodic respiration; great dyspnea, asthmatic breathing. Attacks of spasmodic asthma and most violent spasmodic coughs. He is not able to take a full breath.
Cupr..Sleep: Twitching and jerking and starting during sleep. Grinding of the teeth with brain affections.
Cupr..Sleep, restless: Twitching and jerking and starting during sleep.
Cupr..Stomach: Spasms of the stomach. In the stomach and bowels periodical cramps. Cramps coming periodically. It has pain in the stomach, and a pain under the xiphoid appendix that seems as if it would take his life.
Cupr..Stomach, eructations: Some say it feels as if a lump were in that region and others as if much wind were collected in the stomach.
Cupr..Stomach, pain: Pressure in the stomach. It has pain in the stomach, and a pain under the xiphoid appendix that seems as if it would take his life. If it is not removed he will certainly die in a little while.
Cupr..Abdomen, pain: In young girls beginning to menstruate, violent cramps in the limbs, cramps in the abdomen, diarrhoea, cramps in the uterus. Before or during menses, or after suppression, violent, unbearable cramps in the abdomen.
Cupr..Bladder, urination: Convulsions with suppressed or scanty urine. No urine in the bladder.
Cupr..Generalities, pain: He is full of cramps and is compelled to cry out with the pain from the contractions of the muscles.
Cupr..Generalities, weakness: Great weakness of the muscles, and relaxation of the body when the convulsions are not on.
Cupr..Head, congestion: Violent congestion in the head, violent pains in the head. Congestion of the brain, Meningitis. Instead of convulsions it may take the form of congestion of the brain with violent delirium.
Cupr..Mind, restlessness: Compelled to do something; restless and tossing about a constant uneasiness; nervous trembling; always tired.
Cupr..Appetite: Great thirst for cold drinks. Desire for warm food and drinks. Eats hastily. Indigestion from milk.

Cycl..Chill: Chill not ameliorated by warm clothing. Chilliness during menses. Chill forenoon or evening. Chill predominates in the evening. Heat of face follows chill. Chill and heat alternate.
Cycl..Extremities: Tearing, drawing pains in the limbs. Hyperesthesia of the skin. Flabby muscles. Tearing, drawing in the upper limbs. Sensation of weakness of the hands, as if she must let fall what she holds in her hands. Writer's cramp. Drawing pains in flexors of the leg. Burning sore pain in heels. Toes feel dead after walking. Weakness in the limbs.
Cycl..Generalities: Aversion to motion, yet motion ameliorates her pain and uneasiness. Weakness and aggravation from exertion. Flabby muscles. Most symptoms ameliorated by walking. Very restless at night. Great lassitude. Weakness in the evening, ameliorated by moving about. Sensitive to cold, and cold air. Complaints from being overheated.
Cycl..Genitalia female: Menses too soon, or too late; irregular, or suppressed; copious, prolonged, or scanty. When profuse, the mental symptoms are better. Flow black and clotted. Labor-like pains at the menstrual period, commencing in small of back and running down each side of the pubes. Uterine haemorrhage. Suppressed menses from over-exertion, or being overheated. Fainting at the menstrual period. After menses, milk in mammae.
Cycl..Head: The pain in the head is stunning, and makes her fear she will lose her senses. Boring, darting, pressing in forehead and temples. Violent pains in the forehead. Pains worse lying on painful side or back. Pains one-sided. Pain morning and evening, ameliorated by vomiting; worse by motion and in open air.
Cycl..Mind: Alternations of moods is a striking feature of the mental state. Great flow of ideas alternates with weak memory. Joyous feeling alternating with irritability. Serene humor changes suddenly into seriousness or peevishness. Grief and fear keep her in continuous mental agitation. Dullness of mind prevents mental labor. Absorbed in thought seeks solitude; thinks about the future.
Cycl..Mouth: Taste is lost, or perverted; flat; bad; putrid; rancid; all food tastes too salty. Tongue white, or yellowish. Burning blisters on tongue. Saliva increased. Burning, tip of tongue. Saliva tastes salty. Viscid mucus in the mouth.
Cycl..Rectum: Diarrhoea after coffee. Diarrhoea in chlorotic women subject to sick headaches and menstrual irregularities. Constipation; stool hard. Colic before stool. Colic and urging after stool. Hemorrhoids that bleed. Drawing, pressing pain about the anus, as if a spot would suppurate.
Cycl..Skin: Itching at night in bed. Numbness after scratching, or changing places by scratching.
Cycl..Sleep: Sleep not restful, disturbed by anxious dreams; frightful, vivid dreams. Restless sleep. Late falling asleep. Wakens early, but wants to sleep late. Nightmare. Wakens early, but too tired and sleepy, cannot rise.
Cycl..Stomach: Stomach weak. Nausea after eating. Pork disagrees. The stomach symptoms are much like Pulsatilla. Symptoms worse after coffee. Vomiting after eating. Vomiting in the morning. Vomiting watery mucus. Eructations. Aching in the stomach and burning in oesophagus, better by walking about. Fullness, as if he had eaten too much. Weight in the stomach after eating. Stitching pains in the stomach.
Cycl..Stomach, thirst: Thirstless, except in the evening during fever.
Cycl..Vertigo: Vertigo when walking in the open air; objects turn in a circle; ameliorated in a room, and when sitting. Everything turns dark before the eyes, and she falls as if fainting.
Cycl..Generalities, pain: Stitching pains. Aversion to motion, yet motion ameliorates her pain and uneasiness.
Cycl..Appetite: Loss of appetite and even aversion to food. Satiety after the first mouthful (Lyc.) and then he loathes food.
Cycl..Appetite, aversion: Aversion to bread and butter, and fatty things; but desires inedible things. Disgust for meat.
Cycl..Abdomen: Colicky pains in the abdomen, ameliorated by walking about. Tenderness all over the abdomen, even hypogastrium. Gnawing pains in the evening. Gnawing pains after food. Rumbling and gurgling in the abdomen. Paroxysmal cramps during the night, ameliorated by walking about. Stitching pains in the abdomen and liver.
Cycl..Heart & Circulation: Palpitation and anaemic murmurs. Tumultuous action of the heart; great lassitude. Weak pulse. Stitching in the chest and heart. Tearing, stitching, and shortness of breath during motion and rest.
Cycl..Genitalia female, menses, suppressed: Menses suppressed, palpitation of the heart, weeping, aversion to company and dread of open air. Suppressed menses from over-exertion, or being overheated.
Cycl..Head, pain: The pain in the head is stunning, and makes her fear she will lose her senses. Boring, darting, pressing in forehead and temples. Violent pains in the forehead. Pains worse lying on painful side or back. Pains one-sided. Pain morning and evening, ameliorated by vomiting; worse by motion and in open air. Pressure in vertex, as if brain were enveloped in a cloth, which would deprive him of his senses.
Cycl..Sleep, sleeplessness: Late falling asleep. Wakens early, but too tired and sleepy, cannot rise. Very restless at night.
Cycl..Appetite, wanting: Loss of appetite and even aversion to food.
Cycl..Stomach, indigestion: Pork disagrees. Symptoms worse after coffee. Headaches from disordered stomach.
Cycl..Generalities, pain, pressing: Boring, darting, pressing in forehead and temples. Pressure in vertex, as if brain were enveloped in a cloth, which would deprive him of his senses. Pressing pain over nasal bone during coryza. Drawing, pressing pain about the anus, as if a spot would suppurate. Pressure in the middle of the sternum.
Cycl..Genitalia female, menses: Menses too soon, or too late; irregular, or suppressed; copious, prolonged, or scanty. When profuse, the mental symptoms are better. Flow black and clotted.
Cycl..Mind, sadness: Sadness, as if she had wronged somebody (Aur.). Tearful and meditates upon her grief, which is only imaginary. Thinks she is alone in the world and persecuted by every one (China).

Dros..Back: In these chest complaints, pains between the shoulders, pains in the back as if bruised from coughing.
Dros..Chest: Severe attacks of stitching in the chest when sneezing or coughing. He must press on the chest with the hand for relief. Makes an effort to hold chest with the hand when coughing, the cough is so violent. Compression of the chest, burning sensation in the chest, stitching pains in the chest.
Dros..Chill: Chill and chilliness and one-sided chill. Chill and fever with whooping cough. Chill and fever with inflammation of the larynx.
Dros..Extremities: Spasms of the extremities when coughing. Cramps in the extremities with the cough.
Dros..Extremities, coldness: Coldness in the bands and feet and blueness of the extremities.
Dros..Generalities: When we examine its spasmodic nature, its exhaustion, its cramps, which extend through a large number of complaints, we must realize that it is a more extensive remedy. Epileptiform spasms, prolonged sleeplessness, copious sweat on waking from sleep, restlessness and anxiety.
He must press on the chest with the hand for relief. Makes an effort to hold chest with the hand when coughing, the cough is so violent.
Dros..Head: Lancinating pains in various parts of the body, especially in the head, and must support the head with the hands.
Dros..Mind: imagines that he is constantly persecuted. Anxiety, sleeplessness, and fear of ghosts. Fear of being alone and suspicious of his most intimate friends. Confusion of mind and much dizziness.
Dros..Respiration: Spasmodic difficulties of the chest and larynx cause difficult breathing and suffocation. Asthmatic breathing from talking and constriction in the larynx.
Dros..Respiration, difficult: Difficult breathing especially on waking. Not able to utter a sound. Difficult breathing and suffocating sensation. Sensation as of something in the chest preventing breathing when talking or coughing. Difficult breathing and cough coming after midnight.
Dros..Skin: Corrosive itching in various parts of the body with measles-like eruptions.
Dros..Stomach: Nausea and vomiting. Vomiting of blood and bile in the morning and vomiting of mucus, and food when coughing. Coughing until he retches and vomits.
Dros..Stomach, pain: Constricting pains in the pit of the stomach.
Dros..Abdomen, pain: Constricting pains in the sides of the abdomen. Colic after sour food.
Dros..Generalities, pain: Lancinating pains in various parts of the body, especially in the head, and must support the head with the hands. Stitching pains will be found in many complaints.
Dros..Generalities, pressure: Must support the chest when coughing. Must press upon the abdomen. He must press on the chest with the hand for relief.
Dros..Clinical: It is one of the most frequently indicated remedies for measles. It is a very useful remedy in chronic bronchitis with spasmodic cough. It is a great palliative for the spasmodic cough that occurs in consumption and all along the course of tuberculosis of the lungs.
Dros..Abdomen: Must press upon the abdomen. Constricting pains in the sides of the abdomen. Colic after sour food.
Dros..Back, dorsal region, back: In these chest complaints, pains between the shoulders, pains in the back as if bruised from coughing.
Dros..Back, dorsal region: In these chest complaints, pains between the shoulders, pains in the back as if bruised from coughing.
Dros..Chest, pain: Severe attacks of stitching in the chest when sneezing or coughing. Compression of the chest, burning sensation in the chest, stitching pains in the chest.
Dros..Face: Ordinarily the face is pale and sunken with beat of the face and he has cold extremities, except when coughing, at which time the face becomes red, congested, and purple like Bell. and Cupr.
These attacks of suffocation come on with the cough or come upon lying down. Whooping cough coming in paroxysms of two or three hours, but violently worse after lying down at night and toward 3 o'clock in the morning.
Dros..Head, pain: Lancinating pains in various parts of the body, especially in the head, and must support the head with the hands. Pressive, congestive headaches.
Dros..Generalities, lying: These attacks of suffocation come on with the cough or come upon lying down. Whooping cough coming in paroxysms of two or three hours, but violently worse after lying down at night and toward 3 o'clock in the morning.

Dulc..Back: Rheumatic lameness and stiffness in the back from taking cold better by motion. Drawing pain in the lumber region extending to the lower limbs during rest. Stiff neck from every exposure to cold.
Dulc..Back, pain: Rheumatic lameness and stiffness in the back from taking cold better by motion. Drawing pain in the lumber region extending to the lower limbs during rest.
Dulc..Bladder: In bladder catarrh, where there is a copious discharge of mucus, or muco-pus in the urine; when the urine stands, a thick, purulent sediment, yellowish-white, and a constant urging to urinate; every time he takes a little cold, the urine becomes bloody, the frequency of urination is increased, the urine becomes irritating, the catarrh of the bladder rouses up like a flame, all the symptoms are worse in cold, damp weather, and from getting chilled; better from becoming warm.
Dulc..Chest: The Dulcamara patient often becomes a sickly patient, with threatening of the catarrhal discharges to centre in the bronchial tubes, i.e., in the mucous membrane of the breathing apparatus. Many adults die of acute phthisis that might have been cured by Dulcamara.
Dulc..Chill: fevers may come on, aching in the bones, trembling with the aching, trembling in the muscles, and as the fever goes on, he is in a distressed state... It suits these colds that have this sluggish circulation of the brain, with trembling and chilliness, coldness as if in the bones.
Dulc..Extremities: Stitching, tearing, rheumatic pains in limbs after exposure to cold, better by motion, worse at night or in the evening, with some fever. Sore, bruised feeling all over the body. Warts on hands, fingers and face.
Dulc..Generalities: The Dulcamara patient is disturbed by every change in the weather, from warm to cold, from dry to moist, and from suddenly cooling the body while perspiring. He is ameliorated in dry, even weather; cold and damp aggravate all the conditions. He is worse evening and night and during rest.
Dulc..Genitalia female: Rash comes out upon the face before the menses. As a forerunner of catamenia, with extraordinary sexual excitement, herpetic eruptions.
Dulc..Head: Catarrhal and rheumatic headaches in cold, damp weather. One who is subject to Dulcamara headaches, has the dry catarrh; whenever he takes cold instead of the usual catarrhal flow with it, he at first sneezes and then feels a dryness in the air passages, a slacking up of the usual discharge, which would give him relief, and then he knows that he must look out, for along will come the neuralgic pains, pains in the occiput, and finally over the whole head. Congestive headaches, with neuralgic pains and dry nose.
Dulc..Mind: cannot remember, forgets what he was about to speak of, forgets the word that would naturally express his idea, and he enters into a dazed state, a state of confusion.
Dulc..Mouth: Its cold sores are very troublesome. The patients are subject to these cold sores upon the lips and upon the genitals. Every time he takes cold, herpes labialis, herpes preputialis.
Dulc..Rectum: It is a medicine wonderfully useful in diarrhoea, at the close of the summer, hot days and cold nights, with changeable stool; diarrhoea of infants. There seems to be no digestion; yellow, slimy stool; yellow-green stool, with undigested stool; frequent stool, blood in the stool and quite a mass of slime, showing a marked catarrhal state.
Dulc..Respiration: Cough, from damp, cold, atmosphere, or from getting wet. Cough, dry, hoarse and rough, or loose, with copious expectoration of mucus and dull hearing; catarrhal fever. The cough is worse lying and in a warm room and better in the open air.
Dulc..Skin: Another feature of this medicine is its tendency to throw out eruptions over the body. It is a wonderfully eruptive medicine, producing vesicles, crusts, dry, brown crusts, humid crusts, herpes.
